pattern: A breadth-first sweep from the entrance returns the first border empty cell reached, giving the shortest exit distance.
hints: Shortest steps on an unweighted grid is a breadth-first search problem.
Expand level by level from the entrance, marking visited cells so you never revisit.
An exit is any empty border cell other than the entrance; return the BFS depth when you first step onto one, or -1 if the queue empties.

edge_inputs.description: anchor example exit one step up
anchor example exit two steps right
entrance on border but it does not count
open row with entrance in the middle
single cell entrance equals only cell

stress.description: random grid with mostly open cells and a valid entrance
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(2, size))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(2, size))
    grid = [['.' if rng.random() < 0.7 else '+' for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    er = rng.randrange(m)
    ec = rng.randrange(n)
    grid[er][ec] = '.'
    return [grid, [er, ec]]
